examples: 'stop_desc': 'Beaver St. / Phoenix Ave. (Stop Code: 001) ' -> 'Beaver St. / Phoenix Ave. (Stop Code: 001)'
'stop_desc': 'Beaver St. / Franklin Ave. (Biological Sciences) Southbound (Stop Code: 003) ' -> 'Beaver St. / Franklin Ave. (Biological Sciences) Southbound (Stop Code: 003)'
'stop_desc': 'Beaver St. (Gillenwater Hall) (Stop Code: 004) ' -> 'Beaver St. (Gillenwater Hall) (Stop Code: 004)'
fixes.label: Trimmed surrounding whitespace

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.what: 551 of 551 trips have no headsign.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

findings.fix: Add feed_contact_email to feed_info.txt.
findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (65% marked accessible, 35% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'duplicate_key' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Duplicate key (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Usually an export-tool fix, not hand editing.
findings.fix: Re-generate shape distances in your export; flag to your vendor if it persists.
findings.what: Some trips have stop times whose distances along the route go backwards.
findings.why: Apps can show buses jumping backwards or mis-order stops.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'block_trips_with_overlapping_stop_times' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Block trips with overlapping stop times (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_time_with_arrival_before_previous_departure_time' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop time with arrival before previous departure time (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_diff_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance diff coordinates (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Turn on 'remove unused shapes' (or the like) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already ended.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.effort: A review pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
findings.what: Some stops exist in the feed but no trip ever stops at them.
findings.why: Riders may walk to a stop where no bus is scheduled to arrive.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files leave out fields that GTFS asks for but does not require.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'fast_travel_between_far_stops' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Fast travel between far stops (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
findings.what: A file GTFS asks for (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
findings.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stops_match_shape_out_of_order' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stops match shape out of order (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
findings.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
findings.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_with_shape_dist_traveled_but_no_shape_distances' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip with shape dist traveled but no shape distances (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unsorted_stop_times' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unsorted stop times (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 19 kinds of issue across 785 instances (117 error, 117 warning, 551 informational).

findings.effort: One export when the next period's schedule is set.
findings.fix: Confirm your next service period is published: export its calendar and set feed_info feed_end_date past it.
findings.what: The published calendar ended 190 day(s) ago at a boundary between the feed's own scheduled service periods.
findings.why: The calendar encodes distinct service periods (like academic terms), so this looks like a planned transition rather than a lapse — but trip planners still show nothing until the next period is published.

findings.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
findings.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
findings.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: This feed's calendar reached a scheduled service boundary 190 day(s) ago. Confirm your next service period is published so riders can keep planning trips.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 100% of trips.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 6 validator errors to resolve. Service data expired 188 days ago.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

fetch.user_agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Setting your GTFS agency_id to your five-digit NTD ID is an optional way to line a feed up with its National Transit Database record. FTA links the two on your P-50 form, so it is not a required feed change. We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data expired 188 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

ntd_readiness.summary: Resolve this before you certify on the D-10. 6 validator errors to resolve. Service data expired 188 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 3 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (R005, R008, R066).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 124 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("Beaver / Phoenix", "Beaver / Franklin SB", "Beulah / McConnell", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

findings.effort: A cleanup pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove stops no route serves, or add the trips that should call at them.
findings.what: 11 of 181 boardable stops are never served by any trip.
findings.why: Riders see these stops in apps and on the map but can never catch anything there, which erodes trust in the data.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'duplicate_key' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Duplicate key (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.effort: Usually an export-tool fix, not hand editing.
top_fixes.fix: Re-generate shape distances in your export; flag to your vendor if it persists.
top_fixes.what: Some trips have stop times whose distances along the route go backwards.
top_fixes.why: Apps can show buses jumping backwards or mis-order stops.
